diagrams.title: Transmission Media Classification
diagrams.description: A hierarchical diagram showing the two main categories of transmission media: Guided (wired) and Unguided (wireless), with specific examples for each type. This visual hierarchy helps students understand the complete landscape of how data travels in networks.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental categorization of transmission media and their physical characteristics, helping students visualize the complete spectrum of options available for data transmission in computer networks.

diagrams.title: Analog vs Digital Signals
diagrams.description: A comparison diagram showing the waveform patterns of analog signals (smooth continuous wave) versus digital signals (discrete binary pulses). Includes labels for key properties like amplitude, frequency, and bit intervals.
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates the fundamental difference between analog and digital signals, helping students understand how data is represented and transmitted in different network technologies.

diagrams.title: Transmission Modes
diagrams.description: A diagram demonstrating the three transmission modes: Simplex (one-way), Half-duplex (two-way but alternating), and Full-duplex (two-way simultaneous). Uses directional arrows and device icons to show data flow direction.
diagrams.purpose: Explains the three transmission modes and their practical applications, helping students understand how devices communicate and the efficiency differences between these modes.
